title: Understanding Recommendation Systems

modules.title: How Recommendations Work
modules.sub: Unpacking the mechanics behind AI-driven suggestion engines
modules.description: Learners explore the core algorithms behind recommendation systems, including collaborative filtering, content-based filtering, and hybrid approaches. By examining real-world platform examples, they build a foundational understanding of how user data is collected and translated into personalized suggestions.

modules.title: Personalization and Filtering
modules.sub: How platforms tailor content uniquely to each individual user
modules.description: Learners investigate how platforms construct user profiles and apply filtering techniques to narrow down what each person sees. They assess the trade-offs between relevance and diversity, and consider how personalization decisions are made at scale.

modules.title: Filter Bubbles and Echo Chambers
modules.sub: Recognizing when algorithms limit your exposure to information
modules.description: Learners analyze how persistent personalization can reinforce existing beliefs and reduce exposure to opposing viewpoints or new ideas. They evaluate documented case studies to distinguish between filter bubbles as a technical phenomenon and echo chambers as a social one.

modules.title: Taking Control of Algorithms
modules.sub: Practical strategies to shape and override algorithmic recommendations
modules.description: Learners apply hands-on techniques to audit, adjust, and reset the recommendation signals they send to platforms. They develop a personal action plan for managing their algorithmic footprint across the tools and platforms most relevant to their professional lives.

synopsis: Learn how AI systems recommend content, products, and connections on platforms you use daily. Understand how to influence and control these recommendations.
